records.englishTranslation:
CÜNEYT ALDEMİR (Tokat) - Dear President, Dear Commission members; First of all, I respectfully greet you all.
We had nice Commission meetings over the weekend, where we traveled together in 13 provinces for three or four days. We carried out a series of garden visits and truly well-equipped meetings with non-governmental organizations in Malatya, Adıyaman, Kahramanmaraş, Mersin, Adana, Niğde, Kayseri and Karaman. First of all, I would like to thank all the Commission members who attended this meeting and the valuable participants who both expressed their opinions and honored us by coming. We had really nice and productive meetings.
Of course, did everyone there talk whatever they wanted? I think he talked. We also had friends who didn't talk, they saved their conversations for today, we didn't talk much.
If you realize we didn't talk.
I made a modest 15-20 situation assessments about the most common problems during the field visits there. I want to read them one by one and say them first.
First of all, what our farmers and the common components there want is that our Government should get results as soon as possible after assessing the situation; This is the most important thing. After all, we have thousands of people who are victims. Their damages should be compensated as soon as possible and in the future, what we, as our state, will do here should be determined as soon as possible; This is the main reason.
Secondly, making a regulation regarding TARSİM as a system. It is absolutely necessary for non-insured producers to register with TARSİM and to reorganize the current system. Because although 70 percent of the damages are covered and 10 percent are TARSİM members, unfortunately it is obvious that there is dissatisfaction with TARSİM.
There is a request to postpone the agricultural loans used from Ziraat Bank and Agricultural Credit Cooperatives for a minimum of three years and to obtain new loans in the coming period. Because, as a result, a process related to the recycling of damaged materials will be completed again within the next three years. Naturally, our farmers are asked to be supported by the state during this period.
Of the products that are not included in the product scale, 16 of our products are included in this scale, but we have products such as persimmon and Isparta rose that are not included in the scale. I think that as we travel to other regions, such as Tokat and Amasya in the Black Sea Region, there will be products that are not purchased.
MAYOR ADEM KORKMAZ - Let's establish a liaison with the region.
CÜNEYT ALDEMİR (Tokat) - We have files.
MAYOR ADEM KORKMAZ - No, I mean, if there is anything critical about Tokat or Amasya that is not included but has regional importance, let's include these in our report today. As far as I can reach, I met with Ordu yesterday and said, "We have no problem other than hazelnuts." said the Army people. Let's talk to Manisa, I think there are hazelnuts in Düzce again. Let's contact Manisa.
CÜNEYT ALDEMİR (Tokat) - President, there is no fig here, for example.
İLHAMİ ÖZCAN AYGUN (Tekirdağ) - Tea.
CHAIRMAN ADEM KORKMAZ - Nothing much came about the tea, only a release delay was mentioned.

ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - We are talking about it, let him talk about it, President.
CHAIRMAN ADEM KORKMAZ - I contribute, we will prepare a report, brother, I contribute.
CÜNEYT ALDEMİR (Tokat) - After all, this is a meeting where we impartially explain what we see on the field.
MAYOR ADEM KORKMAZ - Let me leave, brother, you come here!
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - But in my opinion, your party's MP there is saying the right things. When divided, we forget the words.
CÜNEYT ALDEMİR (Tokat) - We will continue, President, do not worry, we have taken our notes.
Apart from that, our farmers have a request that irrigation fees should not be paid at least half or in full because they will not be able to buy crops this year.
Producers who are not registered in the Farmer Registration System are also requested to have their losses caused by agricultural frost disasters evaluated and compensated.
Imports need to be facilitated in order to maintain international connections of vegetable and fruit producers. There is especially high demand from Malatya, Adana and Niğde.
"The fact that the collateral amount of the loans intended to be used and the product costs are equal makes it difficult for the producer to access the loan." There is a demand, there is a process related to it, and there is a demand for it.
Of course, in the places we visited, requests and wishes came about all the problems of Turkish agriculture, not just agricultural frost. Because our farmers were all there together as a common component. There was a meeting held here with the participation of our non-governmental organizations and provincial directorates of agriculture. Here, everyone explained their wishes and wishes in detail, and they were allowed to speak at most.
The destruction of crops due to frost caused agricultural workers to become unemployed. It is requested that working hours be regulated. For example, a situation determination was made that there are 35 thousand people affected by this situation in Niğde.
KGFs are requested to support agricultural pesticide dealers through the use of loans supported by the Credit Support Fund and to provide pharmaceutical support for the next year. This emerged especially in Mersin. Our friends who are agricultural pesticide dealers also expressed their demands regarding this.
In interviews with beekeepers, it was stated that there would be a serious decrease in honey production this year due to the lack of flowering. In other words, I am explaining all of the notes on your behalf so that, at least, any additions can be made in the future.
At the same time, the ÇKS system should be provided to farmers in detail.
It needs to be explained and disseminated. Although their rate has increased with the registration to the Chambers of Agriculture, unfortunately it has not yet reached the desired level. These are the impressions we have received from farmers. Although there is a 10 percent rate in the TARSİM Insurance System and 70 percent of their losses are covered by the state, it has been determined that producers are not registered in the system, and the system needs to be corrected somehow. It is requested that the tax debts of producers affected by agricultural frost be postponed. At the same time, IPARD is requested to support entrepreneurs and especially investments in the field of agricultural production.

It is desired that KGF-supported loans be offered to farmers. At the same time, along with supporting the expenses until April 12, it is also requested to support agricultural production expenditures throughout the year so that the trees can continue productively into the next year. They want the import conditions of Malatya, Nevşehir, Aksaray, Niğde and Karaman provinces to be improved so that the exporting companies can fulfill their commitments - of course, including Malatya. Of course, this frost event created an environment in which no amount of physical work was sufficient, especially during the most difficult times of the last seventy-five years, for twelve hours. Therefore, this is one of the biggest disasters after the earthquake, unprecedented in the history of the republic, because it is a disaster that has severely damaged 35 of our provinces in terms of agriculture. Therefore, I hope our state will do what is necessary regarding this matter.
I tried to explain the impressions we gained from the field here as much as possible. Of course, in the next period, especially when we go to the Black Sea and other regions, in our regions - Amasya, Tokat, likewise - grapes, walnuts, cherries; Since our population is larger, there is a process in which we are not focused on one product. It is estimated that a major frost disaster occurred in Tokat, with an estimated death toll of up to 6.5 billion people; On top of that, we also experienced hail disasters 3 times. For our leaves, for example, we were thinking that we would take the 2nd or 3rd shoot; Then, when there was another hail disaster, we inevitably experienced problems in the process. We want to determine the situation both in the Black Sea and other regions in the next period and how our farmers who were damaged in the process will benefit and how they can sustain their lives in the future; I think we can work on it together.
President, I especially want to thank you. Indeed, we had nice meetings in unity during our weekend trips. I also thank you for your contributions, thank you.

Hafta sonu üç dört günü kapsayan 13 ilimizde hep beraber dolaştığımız güzel Komisyon toplantıları gerçekleştirdik. Malatya, Adıyaman, Kahramanmaraş, Mersin, Adana, Niğde, Kayseri, Karaman'da hem bahçe ziyaretleri hem de sivil toplum örgütleriyle gerçekten donanımlı toplantılar silsilesi gerçekleştirdik. Öncelikle, bu toplantıya katılan tüm Komisyon üyelerimize ve orada hem fikirlerini beyan eden hem de gelerek bizlere onur veren, şeref veren kıymetli katılımcılara da teşekkür ediyorum. Gerçekten güzel ve verimli toplantılar gerçekleştirdik.
Tabii, orada herkes her istediğini konuştu mu? Konuştu bence. Konuşmayan arkadaşlarımız da vardı, konuşmalarını bugüne sakladılar, biz fazla
konuşmadık farkındaysanız.

Ziraat Bankası ve Tarım Kredi Kooperatiflerinden kullanılan tarımsal kredilerin minimum üç yıl süreyle ertelenmesi ve önümüzdeki süreçte de yeniden kredilerin alınmasıyla ilgili bir talep var. Apart from that, our farmers have a request that irrigation fees should not be paid at least half or in full because they will not be able to buy crops this year.
Producers who are not registered in the Farmer Registration System are also requested to have their losses caused by agricultural frost disasters evaluated and compensated.
Imports need to be facilitated in order to maintain international connections of vegetable and fruit producers. There is especially high demand from Malatya, Adana and Niğde.
"The fact that the collateral amount of the loans intended to be used and the product costs are equal makes it difficult for the producer to access the loan." There is a demand, there is a process related to it, and there is a demand for it.
Of course, in the places we visited, requests and wishes came about all the problems of Turkish agriculture, not just agricultural frost. Because our farmers were all there together as a common component. There was a meeting held here with the participation of our non-governmental organizations and provincial directorates of agriculture. Here, everyone explained their wishes and wishes in detail, and they were allowed to speak at most.
The destruction of crops due to frost caused agricultural workers to become unemployed. It is requested that working hours be regulated. For example, a situation determination was made that there are 35 thousand people affected by this situation in Niğde.
KGFs are requested to support agricultural pesticide dealers through the use of loans supported by the Credit Support Fund and to provide pharmaceutical support for the next year. This emerged especially in Mersin. Our friends who are agricultural pesticide dealers also expressed their demands regarding this.
In interviews with beekeepers, it was stated that there would be a serious decrease in honey production this year due to the lack of flowering. In other words, I am explaining all of the notes on your behalf so that, at least, any additions can be made in the future.
At the same time, the ÇKS system should be provided to farmers in detail.
It needs to be explained and disseminated. Although their rate has increased with the registration to the Chambers of Agriculture, unfortunately it has not yet reached the desired level. These are the impressions we have received from farmers. Although there is a 10 percent rate in the TARSİM Insurance System and 70 percent of their losses are covered by the state, it has been determined that producers are not registered in the system, and the system needs to be corrected somehow. It is requested that the tax debts of producers affected by agricultural frost be postponed. At the same time, IPARD is requested to support entrepreneurs and especially investments in the field of agricultural production.

It is desired that KGF-supported loans be offered to farmers. At the same time, along with supporting the expenses until April 12, it is also requested to support agricultural production expenditures throughout the year so that the trees can continue productively into the next year. They want the import conditions of Malatya, Nevşehir, Aksaray, Niğde and Karaman provinces to be improved so that the exporting companies can fulfill their commitments - of course, including Malatya. Of course, this frost event created an environment in which no amount of physical work was sufficient, especially during the most difficult times of the last seventy-five years, for twelve hours. Therefore, this is one of the biggest disasters after the earthquake, unprecedented in the history of the republic, because it is a disaster that has severely damaged 35 of our provinces in terms of agriculture. Therefore, I hope our state will do what is necessary regarding this matter.
I tried to explain the impressions we gained from the field here as much as possible. Of course, in the next period, especially when we go to the Black Sea and other regions, in our regions - Amasya, Tokat, likewise - grapes, walnuts, cherries; Since our population is larger, there is a process in which we are not focused on one product. It is estimated that a major frost disaster occurred in Tokat, with an estimated death toll of up to 6.5 billion people; On top of that, we also experienced hail disasters 3 times. For our leaves, for example, we were thinking that we would take the 2nd or 3rd shoot; Then, when there was another hail disaster, we inevitably experienced problems in the process. We want to determine the situation both in the Black Sea and other regions in the next period and how our farmers who were damaged in the process will benefit and how they can sustain their lives in the future; I think we can work on it together.
President, I especially want to thank you. Indeed, we had nice meetings in unity during our weekend trips. I also thank you for your contributions, thank you.

fields.Subject: Komisyonun bugüne kadar yaptığı toplantılara, zirai don olayından etkilenen illerde yapılan çalışma ziyaretlerine, saha incelemelerine ve Komisyon raporuyla ilgili önerilere ilişkin görüşme

records.originalTurkish:
CÜNEYT ALDEMİR (Tokat) - Sayın Başkan, çok kıymetli Komisyon üyelerimiz; öncelikle teşekkür ederek başlamak istiyorum.
25 ilde gerçekleştirmiş olduğumuz bu ziyaretler kapsamında tüm Komisyon üyesi arkadaşlarımız gerçekten güzel bir katılımla, halktan gelen bütün dertlerimizin tamamını dinleyerek çözüm odaklı çalışmalarımızla inşallah bunları en kısa zamanda sonuçlandırmak üzere büyük bir gayret sarf ettiler. Öncelikle tüm Komisyon üyeleri ve onların nezdinde de Kıymetli Başkanıma teşekkür ediyorum.
Tabii, ülkemiz bir tarım ülkesi ama tarım ülkesi olduğu günden itibaren de her yıl yaşanan ne seller ne dolular ne yangınlar ne de donlar bir şekilde bundan sonraki süreçte de devam edecek ama bizim Zirai Don Komisyonu olarak her gittiğimiz ilde gördüğümüz şey şu: Bunlarla ilgili süreçte çiftçimizin öncelikle kayıt altına alınmasıyla ilgili sürecin hızlandırılması çünkü ÇKS kaydı olmayan hâlâ yüzlerce, binlerce insanımız var. Bunlar bir otokontrol içerisine girmeden ne yapıyorlar, bundan sonraki süreçlerde de ne yapacaklar, bunların illaki bir şekilde kayıt altına alınması lazım. Bundan sonraki süreçte de ÇKS'si olan tüm arkadaşların tamamının da TARSİM'e girmesiyle ilgili ya da sigorta şirketlerinden tazmin edilebilmesiyle ilgili bir sisteme dâhil edilmesi gerekiyor. Biz nasıl arabamızı alıyoruz, arabamızı aldıktan sonra kasko sistemine dâhil ediyoruz, bununla ilgili de şirketlerden az veren, çok veren, az karşılayan, çok karşılayanlar var. Bunları da bir şekilde çiftçimizin idare edip istediği kuruma, kuruluşa bir şekilde sigortasını yaptırması gerekiyor, en başta yaptırmamız gereken şeyin bu olduğuna inanıyorum. Bu TARSİM'le ilgili şikâyetlerin sadece bu kaydolup olmamayla ilgili değil, memnuniyet oranının da çiftçimiz konusunda istenilen düzeyde olmadığı da ortada. Çiftçimizin nasıl TARSİM'e kaydolmasındaki hızı artırabileceğiz
onun yöntemlerine bakmak lazım. TARSİM'in dünyadaki örnekler içerisinde çok fazla başarılı olan sistemlerinin olmadığı da ortada. Yani hangi ülkede TARSİM şirketi olarak yapıldı da başarılı olmuş, çok fazla olduğu ortada değil ama biz bunu da inşallah gerçekleştireceğiz. TARSİM nasıl çiftçimizi daha fazla kayıt yaptırabilecek şekle getirilebiliyorsa onu düzenlememiz gerekir diye düşünüyorum. Şu anda çiftçilerimizin hayatlarını idame edebilmeleri için çekmiş oldukları krediler var, ürünleri yok şu an, ürün olmadığı için bu kredilerin de bir şekilde tanzim edilmesi, önümüzdeki süreçte de hayatlarını idame ettirebilmek için bahçelerine yapacakları masrafların da bir şekilde tedarik edilebilmesi için de bu sene çekecekleri yeni krediler için de ya faizsiz ya da onunla ilgili de bir düzenleme yapılıp en azından önümüzdeki süreçlerde bu maddi hasarların ve kayıpların bir şekilde çiftçilerimizin ayakta kalabilmesi için düzenlenmesi gerektiğine inanıyorum. Zirai donla ilgili önümüzdeki süreç de; bu yüzyıllar boyu devam edecek, bu sonuçta bir doğal afet yani bugün son yetmiş beş yılda olmayan bir zirai don olayı yaşadık, bundan sonra da bunu yaşayacağız. Önümüzdeki süreçte bizim bahçelerimizde nasıl önlem almamızla ilgili yapacağımız faaliyetlere de önayak olabilmek için bu işleri teşvik etmemiz lazım. Yani işte, rüzgâr gülleri gibi bunları yapacak yüksek şeyleri, örtüleme teknikleri, ne gerekiyorsa bunun ilmî olarak araştırılıp bölgesel bazda bunların desteklenmesi gerektiğine inanıyorum ve aynı zamanda da gittiğimiz yerde gördük ki oranın doğal şartlarına uymayan, işte su yok, biz bir şekilde orada tarımsal faaliyet yapıyoruz. Onun dışında, ürün yelpazesi olarak dona elverişli olan iklim şartları olan coğrafi yerler var, orada dona hassas olan, zirai dona hassas olan ürünler yetiştiriyoruz. Bölgesel bazlı olarak yapacağımız ürünlerin de muhakkak coğrafi şartlara uyan şekilde ürün yelpazelerinin değiştirilmesi gerektiğine de inanıyoruz, buradan çıkan sonuçlardan biri de bu. Etkilenen sektörler sadece çiftçiler değil, çiftçilerin haricinde zirai ilaç satan arkadaşlar var, arıcılar var, hayvancılıkla uğraşan insanlar var, çiftlikler var, bunların da bu süreçte etkilenmelerinin daha aza indirilmesi için de bunlara da kredi imkânlarının sağlanıp inşallah süreçlerin desteklenmesi gerektiğine de inanıyorum. Tabii, bunun dışında daha çok not alınacak şeyler var ama sahada 25 ili gezdiğimiz zaman gördüğümüz izlenimler bunlar. İnşallah, Zirai Don Komisyonu olarak gezdiğimiz her yerde de beklentilerin çok yüksek olduğunu da biz izlemledik, onun için de bu sonuçların bir an önce nihayete kavuşması için Komisyon olarak da inşallah bu süreci de hızlandırıp bir an önce çiftçimizin eline ne geçecekse onu tedarik etmenin de yollarına bakmamız lazım diye düşünüyorum.
Ben bu Komisyon sırasında bizlere eşlik eden tüm Meclis çalışanlarına da, kıymetli Komisyon üyelerimize de ve Kıymetli Başkanımıza da teşekkür ediyorum. Bizleri oralarda misafir eden çok kıymetli illerdeki bürokratlara, çiftçilerimize de geçmiş olsun dileklerimi iletmek istiyorum.
Teşekkür ediyorum, sağ olun.
records.englishTranslation:
CÜNEYT ALDEMİR (Tokat) - Mr. President, our very valuable Commission members; First of all, I would like to start by saying thank you.
Within the scope of these visits that we carried out in 25 provinces, all our Commission members participated with a really good participation, listened to all our problems from the public and made a great effort to conclude them as soon as possible with our solution-oriented work. First of all, I would like to thank all the Commission members and my esteemed President.
Of course, our country is an agricultural country, but the floods, hail, fires and frosts that have occurred every year since the day it became an agricultural country will somehow continue in the future, but what we, as the Agricultural Frost Commission, see in every province we visit is this: In the process related to these, the process of registering our farmers should be accelerated, because we still have hundreds, thousands of people who are not registered with the ÇKS. What they do without any self-control and what they will do in the future must be recorded in some way. In the next period, all friends with ÇKS must be included in a system that allows them to enter TARSİM or be compensated by insurance companies. Just as we buy our car and include it in the insurance system after purchasing it, there are companies that pay less, give more, cover less, and cover more. Our farmers need to manage these in some way and get insurance from the institution or organization they want, I believe this is what we should have done in the first place. It is obvious that the complaints about TARSİM are not only about whether to register or not, but also that the satisfaction rate is not at the desired level regarding our farmers. How can we increase the speed of our farmers' registration to TARSİM?
We need to look at his methods. It is obvious that TARSİM does not have many successful systems among the examples in the world. In other words, it is not clear in which country it was established as a TARSİM company and whether it was successful or not, but hopefully we will achieve this. I think we should regulate TARSİM in the same way that it can enable our farmers to register more. Currently, there are loans that our farmers have taken to survive, but they do not have any products at the moment. Since there is no product, these loans should be arranged in some way, and in order to provide the expenses they will incur for their gardens in order to continue their lives in the coming period, I believe that the new loans they will take this year should be either interest-free or an arrangement related to it should be made, and at least in the coming periods, these financial damages and losses should be regulated in some way so that our farmers can survive. The upcoming period regarding agricultural frost; This will continue for centuries, this is ultimately a natural disaster, that is, today we have experienced an agricultural frost event that has not occurred in the last seventy-five years, and we will experience this from now on. We need to encourage these activities in the coming period in order to be a pioneer in the activities we will do about how to take precautions in our gardens.

So, I believe that high things such as weather vanes, covering techniques, whatever is necessary should be researched scientifically and supported on a regional basis, and at the same time, wherever we went, we saw that it does not comply with the natural conditions of the place, there is no water, we are somehow carrying out agricultural activities there. Apart from that, there are geographical places with climate conditions suitable for frost in terms of product range, where we grow frost-sensitive and agricultural products that are sensitive to frost. We also believe that the product ranges of the products we will produce on a regional basis should be changed to suit the geographical conditions, and this is one of the results. The affected sectors are not only farmers, other than farmers, there are friends who sell pesticides, there are beekeepers, there are people engaged in animal husbandry, there are farms. I also believe that in order to reduce their impact in this process, credit opportunities should be provided to them and hopefully the processes should be supported. Of course, there are more things to note than this, but these are the impressions we saw when we visited 25 provinces in the field. Hopefully, as the Agricultural Don Commission, we observed that the expectations were very high everywhere we visited, so in order for these results to be finalized as soon as possible, I think that as the Commission, we need to accelerate this process and look at ways to supply whatever our farmers will receive as soon as possible.
I would like to thank all the Parliament staff, our esteemed Commission members and our esteemed President who accompanied us during this Commission. I would like to convey my best wishes to the bureaucrats and farmers in the very valuable provinces that hosted us there.
Thank you, thank you.

The affected sectors are not only farmers, other than farmers, there are friends who sell pesticides, there are beekeepers, there are people engaged in animal husbandry, there are farms. I also believe that in order to reduce their impact in this process, credit opportunities should be provided to them and hopefully the processes should be supported. Of course, there are more things to note than this, but these are the impressions we saw when we visited 25 provinces in the field. Hopefully, as the Agricultural Don Commission, we observed that the expectations were very high everywhere we visited, so in order for these results to be finalized as soon as possible, I think that as the Commission, we need to accelerate this process and look at ways to supply whatever our farmers will receive as soon as possible.
I would like to thank all the Parliament staff, our esteemed Commission members and our esteemed President who accompanied us during this Commission. I would like to convey my best wishes to the bureaucrats and farmers in the very valuable provinces that hosted us there.
Thank you, thank you.

records.title: Komisyon raporunda yer verilecek hususlara ilişkin görüş, öneri ve değerlendirmeler ile çalıştay raporları hakkında görüşme

fields.Subject: Komisyon raporunda yer verilecek hususlara ilişkin görüş, öneri ve değerlendirmeler ile çalıştay raporları hakkında görüşme

records.originalTurkish:
CÜNEYT ALDEMİR (Tokat) - Başkanım,
teşekkür ediyorum.
Öncelikle, sizin nezdinizde tüm Komisyon üyelerimize, kıymetli milletvekili arkadaşlarıma, Komisyon sırasında tüm saha ziyaretlerine katılan basın mensuplarımıza, Meclisin çalışanlarına, oralarda bizi misafir eden çok kıymetli çiftçilerimize, oradaki idari personele teşekkür ediyorum.
14 Mayıs 2025'te başlamış olduğumuz Zirai Don Komisyonuna gerçekten sadece zirai donun tespiti değil, aynı zamanda, Türkiye'nin tarım politikalarıyla da ilgili güzel bir vizyon açmasından dolayı teşekkür ediyorum. Önderliğinizde gerçekten güzel bir Komisyon çalışması yaptık ve bu Komisyon çalışmasının sonucunda da 46,5 milyar gibi devasa bir rakam çiftçimizin üretimine devam edebilmesi için bir can suyu olarak inşallah onların ürünlerine destek olarak verilecek. Tabii, burada emeği geçen çok kıymetli Cumhurbaşkanımıza, Tarım Bakanımıza ve Komisyon üyelerimize ve nezdinizde de sizlere teşekkür ediyorum. Bu birliğimizin, beraberliğimizin hem devam etmesi hem de bundan sonraki süreçte Türkiye'nin tarım politikalarıyla ilgili, iklimle ilgili gelişmeler, TARSİM'le ilgili gelişmeler, bunlar, tamamen Komisyonumuzun vermiş olduğu raporlar doğrultusunda da inşallah güzel sonuçlar elde edeceğimize inanıyorum.
Gerçekten, sahada da Komisyonumuz olarak insanlarımızın arasında, çiftçilerimizin arasında gördüklerimiz, göremediklerimiz, bir sürü konuya temas ettik, bunları ilettik. Bu raporlar doğrultusunda da inşallah Komisyonumuz Tarım Bakanlığımız nezdinde ve Türkiye'mizin gelişmesi nezdinde de güzel sonuçlar alacağına inanıyorum. Gönül istiyor ki bundan sonraki süreçte de bu Komisyonumuzun bir şekilde devam edip sonuçlarının inşallah üreticimize bir şekilde ulaşmasını arzu ediyorum. Bu desteklemelerin yanında da özellikle Komisyon olarak bizim istemiş olduğumuz bir şey daha var. Önümüzdeki süreçte olur mu bilmiyorum ama tarımsal kredilerimizin en azından bu sene içerisinde ötelenmesiyle de ilgili çiftçimizin yoğun talebi vardı. Yani bunun da inşallah gerçekleşmesiyle ilgili süreç olumlu olarak yansırsa en azından çiftçimize birazcık daha fazla can suyunun geleceğiyle ilgili fikrimizi de bildirmek istiyorum kayıtlara geçmesi için. Sonuçta, Türkiye ekonomik sıkıntılar içerisinde ama bunu inşallah hep beraber el ele verip atlatacağız. Az önce Ayhan Bey'in dediği gibi, 2025 yılı gerçekten doğal afetler olarak çiftçimizin sıkıntıya mustarip olduğu bir yıl olarak geçti. Sadece zirai don değil, işte, seller, felaketler, fırtınalar, kuraklıklar, son elli yılın en kurak yazını yaşadık. Bunların tamamı biriktiği zaman tarımsal sektörde ister istemez sıkıntılarımızın olduğu bir süreç yaşadık. İnşallah, devletimizin desteğiyle bu süreçleri sıkıntısız bir şekilde atlatmanın yollarını hep beraber arayacağız diyorum.
Komisyonda çalışan tüm milletvekili arkadaşlarımıza ve sizlere teşekkür ediyorum. İnşallah daha güzel hizmetlerin ve görüşlerin sunulacağı komisyonlarda buluşmak dileğiyle diyorum, hepinizi saygıyla selamlıyorum.
Sağ olun.
records.englishTranslation:
CÜNEYT ALDEMİR (Tokat) - President,
thank you
First of all, I would like to thank all our Commission members, my dear fellow MPs, our press members who participated in all field visits during the Commission, the staff of the Assembly, our very valuable farmers who hosted us there, and the administrative staff there.
I would like to thank the Agricultural Frost Commission, which we started on May 14, 2025, for not only detecting agricultural frost but also opening a beautiful vision regarding Türkiye's agricultural policies. We have carried out a really good Commission study under your leadership, and as a result of this Commission study, a huge figure of 46.5 billion will hopefully be given to our farmers as a lifeline to support their products so that they can continue their production. Of course, I would like to thank our esteemed President, our Minister of Agriculture, our Commission members, and you, who contributed here. I believe that we will achieve good results in the future, in line with the reports given by our Commission, regarding Türkiye's agricultural policies, climate-related developments, TARSİM-related developments, and the continuation of this unity and solidarity.
Indeed, as our Commission, we touched on many issues in the field, what we saw and what we could not see among our people, our farmers, and conveyed them. In line with these reports, I believe that our Commission will achieve good results in the eyes of our Ministry of Agriculture and in the development of our Turkey. I sincerely hope that this Commission will continue in some way in the future and hopefully the results will reach our producers in some way. In addition to these supports, there is something else that we, especially as the Commission, want. I don't know if it will happen in the coming period, but there was a strong demand from our farmers for our agricultural loans to be postponed at least this year. So, hopefully, if the process of this happening is reflected positively, I would like to express our opinion that at least a little more lifeline will come to our farmers, for the record. After all, Türkiye is in economic difficulties, but hopefully we will get through this together. As Mr. Ayhan just said, the year 2025 was a year in which our farmers suffered from natural disasters. Not only agricultural frost, but also floods, disasters, storms, droughts, we experienced the driest summer of the last fifty years. When all of these accumulated, we inevitably experienced a period of problems in the agricultural sector. Hopefully, with the support of our state, we will all look for ways to get through these processes without any problems.
I would like to thank you and all our parliamentary friends working in the commission. I hope to meet you in commissions where better services and opinions will be offered, and I greet you all with respect.
Thank you.
